---
engine: julia
execute: 
  cache: false 
julia:
  env: ["QUARTO_JULIA_PROJECT=."]
mainfont: Lato
fig-align: center
format:
  agil_stcalc_template-typst: default
company: Agilitus
client: Glencore
proj_no: 26016
proj_title: Glencore Port C2A Conveyor Relocation Engineering
calc_no: 26016-ST-CAL-0003
calc_title: Retaining wall check
proj_phase: Detailed Design
logo_company: "images/logo-agilitus.png"
logo_client: "images/logo-MIM-glencore.png"
revisions:
- rev_no: A
  rev_date: today
  rev_desc: IFIR 
  rev_prep: Josua A
  rev_check: F. Hakim
  rev_app:  
---

```{=typst}
#like_header("Calculation Objective / Scope")
```
* Check existing retaining wall stability


```{=typst}
#like_header("Calculation Assumptions")
```
* Allowable bearing pressure, 200kPa (assumed, after years of preloading, considering its a granular fill based on the geotech report)


```{=typst}
#like_header("Software Used")
```
* n/a

```{=typst}
#like_header("Holds")
```
* n/a

{{< pagebreak >}}

```{=typst}
#block(above: 2em, below: 2em)[
    #outline(
      title: [Table of Contents],
      depth: 3,
      indent: auto
    );
    ]
```

{{< pagebreak >}}

```{julia}
#| label: Usings
#| echo: false
#| output: false

import Pkg
Pkg.activate(".")

using Unitful, Unitful.DefaultSymbols
using MyUnits, RetWalls, SoilModule, ConcreteModule
using MyHelper: _println
```

# Introduction

## Basis of Design									

In general, design shall be in accordance with AS 4678 and other relevant standards.

## Design Standards and Codes

Unless specifically noted, the design complies with Australian Standards and selected international standards as listed in the Design Criteria. The following summarises the key standards used in producing this calculation:

* AS 1170.0 - 2002		Structural design actions - Part 0: General principles
* AS 1170.1 - 2002		Structural design actions - Part 1: Permanent, imposed and other actions   
* AS 1170.4 - 2002		Structural design actions - Part 4: Earthquake actions  
* AS 4678 - 2002		Retaining structures
* AS 3600 - 2018		Concrete structures

## Other Referenced Documents

- Report on Geotechnical Investigation, Proposed Concentrate Storage Shed Extension, September 2009. (pdf filename: 50805 - PORT OF TOWNSVILLE, Proposed Shed Extension, Ernest Henry Mining)

## Bulk Material

* Bulk material density, 1900kg/m3 (Zinc concentrate) and 2530kg/m3 (Lead concentrate). 
* Bulk material angle of friction, 30 to 60 degrees.
* backfill slope is 36 degrees, as per Navis model, which refers to the most critical scenario given the limit on stockpile height.

## Methodology

Calculate wall stability according to AS4678 by manual calculation. The stability analysis is carreid out for the range of friction angles. The backfill surface is kept constant, per Navis model.
 
# Wall dimensions

<!-- some double column figures -->
::: {#fig-builtup_loads layout-ncol=2}

![Stockpile height](images/backfill_geometry_stockpile_height.png){width="350"}

![Backfill at Wall height](images/backfill_geometry_wall_height.png){width="350"}

![Backfill slope](images/bakfill_slope.png){width="350"}

Backfill details
:::


![Reinforced wall details](images/rc_wall_details.png){#fig-rc_wall_details width="400"}

```{julia} 
#|output: false 
T_footing = 0.45m
H_wall = 5950mm - T_footing # from drg 595-3-5880

H_wall_above_floor = 5.392m # as measured in scan, top of wall to floor level
H_backfill_from_floor = 4.1m # as measured in scan
H_floor_to_tof = H_wall - H_wall_above_floor

H_backfill = H_backfill_from_floor + H_floor_to_tof
B_footing = 4m
Tw_bottom = 0.45m
Tw_top = 0.2m
X_frontwallface = 0.4m
H_soil_on_toe = H_floor_to_tof
Fill_slope = (atand(3858 / 5350))° |> f2  # refer figure above
d_peak_slope = 5350mm # distance from wall to peak of slope
```

# Overturning Stability and Bearing Check 

Define foundation soil:

```{julia} 
q_allow_drained = 200kPa  # taken as current, given years of preloading.
subsoil = Soil_AS4678(; γ=18kN / m^3, ϕi=30.0°, Φn=1.0, Φd=0.9, Φuϕ=0.85)
```

Define backfill material and wall object, no partial factors.

```{julia}
backfills = [Soil_AS4678(; γ=2530kgf / m^3, ϕi=ϕ * 1°, δ=ϕ * 1°, Φn=1.0, Φd=1.0, Φuϕ=1.0) for ϕ in 30:1:60]
retwalls = [CantileverRetWall(B_footing=B_footing, H_wall=H_wall, H_backfill=H_backfill, T_footing=T_footing, Tw_bottom=Tw_bottom, Tw_top=Tw_top, X_frontwallface=X_frontwallface, H_toesoil=H_soil_on_toe, β=Fill_slope, subsoil=subsoil, backfill=backfill, A=0.0m, B=d_peak_slope) for backfill in backfills];
```

```{julia}
# using unfactored material parameters
LF_wsd = LoadFactors(γDC=1.0,
    γEV=1.0, # vertical component of earth pressure
    γEH=1.0, # horizontal component of earth pressure
    γEQE=0.0 # earthquake load
)
wall_actions = [fWallStability(retwall, LF_wsd) for retwall in retwalls];

σvs = [t.σv for t in wall_actions]
(σvs_max, i) = findmax(σvs)
println(wall_actions[i].calc_details)
```

Critical backfill data the produces the highest bearing pressure:

```{julia}
#| echo: false 
display(_println(backfills[i]))
```

```{julia}
#| echo: false 
plot_retwall_geometry(retwalls[i], wall_actions[i].ρa)
```

THe maximum bearing pressure is `{julia} (σvs_max |> f2)`, `{julia} σvs_max ≤ q_allow_drained ? "≤ $(q_allow_drained), PASS" : " > $(q_allow_drained) FAIL"`

# Sliding Check
- wall is locked in by slab, no further analysis for sliding is required.

# Wall reinforcement check  

Define backfill material and wall object, with typical insitu material partial factors:

```{julia}
backfills_uls = [Soil_AS4678(; γ=2530kgf / m^3, ϕi=ϕ * 1°, δ=ϕ * 1°, Φn=1.0, Φd=0.9, Φuϕ=0.85) for ϕ in 30:1:60]

retwalls_uls = [CantileverRetWall(B_footing=B_footing, H_wall=H_wall, H_backfill=H_backfill, T_footing=T_footing, Tw_bottom=Tw_bottom, Tw_top=Tw_top, X_frontwallface=X_frontwallface, H_toesoil=H_soil_on_toe, β=Fill_slope, subsoil=subsoil, backfill=backfill, A=0.0m, B=d_peak_slope) for backfill in backfills_uls];
```

## Load combination case 1: Dead + Live loads, no seismic:

Apply load factors for ULS methods:

```{julia}
LF_uls = LoadFactors(γDC=1.2,
    γEV=1.2, # vertical component of earth pressure, 1.2 instead of 1.5 per AS 4678.
    γEH=1.2, # horizontal component of earth pressure
    γEQE=0.0 # earthquake load
)

# determine design actions to be used later.
wall_actions_uls = [fWallStability(retwall, LF_uls; kh=kh) for retwall in retwalls_uls];
```

## Load combination case 1: Dead + Live loads + seismic:

```{julia}
kp = 1.0
Z = 0.09 # hazard factor for site, same as peak groundacceleration
a = kp * Z # peak ground acceleration

ah = 0.5 * a # Appendix I.13.a AS4678
az = 0.0

# where amplification in the strucutre is not relevant 
kh = ah # I14(3)
kv = 0.0; # I14(4)

LF_uls_seismic = LoadFactors(γDC=1.0,
    γEV=1.0, # vertical component of earth pressure, 1.2 instead of 1.5 per AS 4678.
    γEH=1.0, # horizontal component of earth pressure
    γEQE=1.0 # earthquake load
)

wall_actions_uls_seismic = [fWallStability(retwall, LF_uls_seismic; kh=kh) for retwall in retwalls_uls];
```

## Shear and moment calculation

Combine results for seismic and non seismic cases, and find critical case for moment and shear

```{julia}
retwall_all = vcat(retwalls_uls, retwalls_uls)
wall_actions_all = vcat(wall_actions_uls, wall_actions_uls_seismic)

y = 0m  # critical moment, from top of footing 
res_vm = [fWallShearandMoment(y, retwall, shear_and_moment) for (retwall, shear_and_moment) in zip(retwall_all, wall_actions_all)]
Mu = [t.my for t in res_vm]
Vu = [t.vy for t in res_vm]

Mu_max, i_max = findmax(Mu)
Vu_max = Vu[i_max]
println(wall_actions_uls[i_max].calc_details)
```

```{julia}
#| echo: false
println(res_vm[i_max].calc_details)
```


Define concrete section/capacity:

```{julia}
D = retwalls[1].Twbot
γc = 24.0kN / m^3;
db = 20mm;
Ast = π / 4 * db^2;
# cover to centre of rebar
cc = 50mm + 1.5 * db;
d = D - cc;
# shear ties
Asv_per_tiespacing = 0.0mm;

# define slab, in multiple of bar spacing, 960mm = 8 x 120mm
rc_wall = AS3600RectSection(fc=32MPa, fy=500MPa, b=0.96m, d=d, D=D, nbb=8, Abb=Ast, nbt=5, Abt=Ast)
rc_wall.concrete_section.plot_section(filename="images/rc_wall_sec.png");
```

![](images/rc_wall_sec.png){width="50%"} 
 
```{julia}
#| echo: false
Mdes_rc, str_o = fdesign_beam_moment(rc_wall, Mu=Mu_max)
println(str_o)
```

For shear, max taken at d distance from top of footing:

```{julia}
y = 450mm  # critical moment, from top of footing for shear
res_vm = [fWallShearandMoment(y, retwall, shear_and_moment) for (retwall, shear_and_moment) in zip(retwalls_uls, wall_actions_uls)]
Mu = [t.my for t in res_vm]
Vu = [t.vy for t in res_vm]

Mu_max, i_max = findmax(Mu)
Vu_max = Vu[i_max]
println(wall_actions_uls[i_max].calc_details)
```

```{julia}
#| echo: false
println(res_vm[i_max].calc_details)
```

```{julia}
#| echo: false
u, str_o = fdesign_beam_shear(rc_wall, Vu_max, Mu_max, 0kN / m; override_ks=false, ignore_min=true)
println(str_o)
```
